content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5660

Administration
322-2660

Executive/Legal
322-5901

Conflict of Interest
322-6441
322-6444

March 4, 1980


Ms. Ruth H. Harris
2420 Bennington Drive
San Bruno, CA 94066

A-80-03-015

Dear Ms. Harris:

Thank you for your letter dated February 15, 1980 seeking
informal advice regarding the disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act. The facts as I understand them are
as follows.

You are a candidate for the City Council of San Bruno
and must therefore file a Statement of Economic Interests
with your declaration of candidacy. You were informed by
the City Clerk's office that this Statement need not contain
your interests in real property which are more than two miles
outside the City limits. You now ask that our office confirm
this advice.

The Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 87200,
et seq., requires you to file a candidate's statement disclosing
investments and interests in real property located within the
jurisdiction of the office you seek. The Act defines those
circumstances when real property is considered to be in the
jurisdiction as follows:

Real property shall be deemed to be 'within the
jurisdiction'. . . if the property or any part of
it is located within or not more than two miles
outside the boundaries of the jurisdiction or
within two miles of any land owned or used by the
local government agency.

Government Code Section 82035
(Emphasis added.)

Ms. Ruth H. Harris                                    Page Two
March 4, 1980


     You must therefore disclose any interests in real
property you have within the San Bruno City limits, within
two miles of the City limits, and within two miles of any
land, if any, outside the City limits which is nevertheless
used by the City of San Bruno. Any other interest in real
property you have need not be disclosed.

     I hope that this advice is helpful. If I can be of
further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Legal Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DED:nrw

2420 Bennington Drive
San Bruno, CA 94066

February 15, 1980

F P P C
FEB 20 & 30 AM '80

F.E.P.C.
1100 K Street
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804


Gentlemen:

I was given your offices to write to for an opinion, after
I called the Attorney-General's office in San Francisco,
regarding an interpretation of the law. I refer to the
Property Disclosure Law regarding office-seekers in a
local (city) election.

I am presently running for the office of Council member in
San Bruno. I was told that it is not necessary to disclose
property which you might have that would be over two miles
from the city wherein one is running for office. This information came from the official person with whom we file
our papers.

Please let me have your interpretation of this ruling.
Is this correct?

Sincerely yours,

RUTH H. HARRIS
